meaning: n.失事船只,破坏vt.完全毁坏
examples: Of the original scheme hardly a wrack remains.
The wrack of the city was still hot from the fire.
They've been in a very terrible trolley car wrack .
But an own goal from Ian Roper ensured penalties and Darren Wrack's miss ultimately proved crucial.
Wrack does not feel it is time for a change although the club are bottom of League One.
